## Developer Guide

If you are new to using `nbdev` here are some useful pointers to get you
started.

### Install ejemplo_2026_nbdev in Development mode

``` sh
# make sure ejemplo_2026_nbdev package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to ejemplo_2026_nbdev
$ nbdev_prepare
```

## Usage

### Installation

Install latest from the GitHub
[repository](https://github.com/msalazarcgeo/ejemplo_2026_nbdev):

``` sh
$ pip install git+https://github.com/msalazarcgeo/ejemplo_2026_nbdev.git
```

or from [conda](https://anaconda.org/msalazarcgeo/ejemplo_2026_nbdev)

``` sh
$ conda install -c msalazarcgeo ejemplo_2026_nbdev
```

or from [pypi](https://pypi.org/project/ejemplo_2026_nbdev/)

``` sh
$ pip install ejemplo_2026_nbdev
```

### Documentation

Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub
[repository](https://github.com/msalazarcgeo/ejemplo_2026_nbdev)’s
[pages](https://msalazarcgeo.github.io/ejemplo_2026_nbdev/).
Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on
[conda](https://anaconda.org/msalazarcgeo/ejemplo_2026_nbdev) and
[pypi](https://pypi.org/project/ejemplo_2026_nbdev/) respectively.
